name: Brandenburg Gate
summary: The Brandenburg Gate is an 18th-century neoclassical monument in Berlin and one of Germany's most iconic landmarks. Built between 1788 and 1791 by order of King Frederick William II of Prussia, it stands at the western end of Unter den Linden boulevard. The gate has witnessed Napoleon's triumphal march, survived two World Wars, stood at the heart of Cold War division, and became the supreme symbol of German reunification in 1989.

timeline.title: Completion of the Gate
timeline.description: The Brandenburg Gate is completed after three years of construction, designed by architect Carl Gotthard Langhans in the style of the Propylaea of the Acropolis in Athens. It is crowned by the Quadriga, a chariot drawn by four horses and driven by the goddess of victory, designed by Johann Gottfried Schadow.

timeline.title: Napoleon Seizes the Quadriga
timeline.description: After defeating Prussia at the Battle of Jena-Auerstedt, Napoleon enters Berlin through the Brandenburg Gate and orders the Quadriga sculpture dismantled and shipped to Paris as a war trophy. The gate symbolizes Prussian humiliation and the loss of national pride.

timeline.title: Quadriga Returns from Paris
timeline.description: Following Napoleon's defeat, the Quadriga is triumphantly returned to Berlin from Paris. The goddess of victory is redesigned to include an Iron Cross and Prussian eagle, transforming her from a symbol of peace into one of military triumph.

timeline.title: Nazi Torchlight Processions
timeline.description: The Nazis hold massive torchlight processions through the Brandenburg Gate to celebrate Hitler's seizure of power. The gate becomes a central stage for Nazi propaganda events and a symbol of the Third Reich's militaristic ambitions.

timeline.title: Battle of Berlin Destruction
timeline.description: During the final Battle of Berlin in World War II, the Brandenburg Gate is heavily damaged by Soviet artillery and infantry fighting. The Quadriga is almost completely destroyed, with only one horse's head surviving. The gate stands as a haunting symbol of a shattered city.

timeline.title: Isolated by the Berlin Wall
timeline.description: The construction of the Berlin Wall leaves the Brandenburg Gate stranded in the restricted zone between East and West Berlin, inaccessible to citizens of either side. It becomes the most powerful symbol of the Cold War division and the global struggle between communism and democracy.

timeline.title: Fall of the Berlin Wall
timeline.description: On November 9, 1989, the Berlin Wall is opened and hundreds of thousands of jubilant Germans stream through the Brandenburg Gate, celebrating reunification. The gate transforms overnight from a symbol of division into the supreme emblem of freedom and unity in Europe.

images.caption: The newly completed Brandenburg Gate with the original peaceful Quadriga crowning its summit
Napoleon's troops parading through the gate after the conquest of Prussia
The Brandenburg Gate stranded in no-man's-land behind the newly erected Berlin Wall
Celebrations at the Brandenburg Gate as Germans reunite on November 9, 1989
